    "R1.1": "The section makes a contribution claim (bias elimination, human-alignment generalization) and frames a gap (self-preference in LLM judges); verifying whether the framing is accurate and the contribution precisely scoped is a central review responsibility.",
    "R1.2": "Several inferential moves need checking: the leap from a single (judge, corpus) instantiation to the general claim of elimination 'across model families and judge tasks'; whether the 0.05 residual gap logically supports 'eliminates'; and whether the 52% pairwise rate adequately supports the null-rate claim.",
    "R1.3": "The section is dense with quantitative claims tied to specific experimental results (mean scores, kappa values, pairwise rates, latency). Verifying that each stated number is faithfully supported by the described experiment — and that no number is cherry-picked or misattributed — is load-bearing for this review.",
    "R1.4": "The paper positions DebiasJudge as novel; a review should assess whether the provenance-anonymizing prompt and learned bias-correction term represent a genuine delta over prior debiasing or calibration work in LLM evaluation literature."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review assess writing quality and structural presentation (R1.5/R1.6) as well as technical content, or is it scoped solely to the correctness and validity of the scientific claims and experimental evidence?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The section makes a contribution claim (bias elimination, human-alignment generalization) and frames a gap (self-preference in LLM judges); verifying whether the framing is accurate and the contribution precisely scoped is a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.2: Several inferential moves need checking: the leap from a single (judge, corpus) instantiation to the general claim of elimination 'across model families and judge tasks'; whether the 0.05 residual gap logically supports 'eliminates'; and whether the 52% pairwise rate adequately supports the null-rate claim.
rationale.R1.3: The section is dense with quantitative claims tied to specific experimental results (mean scores, kappa values, pairwise rates, latency). Verifying that each stated number is faithfully supported by the described experiment — and that no number is cherry-picked or misattributed — is load-bearing for this review.
rationale.R1.4: The paper positions DebiasJudge as novel; a review should assess whether the provenance-anonymizing prompt and learned bias-correction term represent a genuine delta over prior debiasing or calibration work in LLM evaluation literature.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review assess writing quality and structural presentation (R1.5/R1.6) as well as technical content, or is it scoped solely to the correctness and validity of the scientific claims and experimental evidence?
